channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery Over Iranian Oil Purchases
item.description: The Trump administration sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries, along with 40 shipping companies for transporting Iranian oil. The move is part of a "maximum pressure" campaign to cut off Iran's key revenue source ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping.

item.title: Pentagon Email Floats Suspending Spain From NATO Over Iran Stance
item.description: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies who failed to support US operations against Iran, including suspending Spain and reviewing the US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim. NATO officials say Trump cannot unilaterally expel members.

item.title: Trump Announces 3-Week Extension of Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks after White House talks. The truce pauses fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, though both sides exchanged fire shortly after the announcement.

item.title: China's Hengli Petrochemical Denies Trading With Iran After US Sanctions
item.description: Hengli Petrochemical, one of China's largest independent refiners, denied allegations from the US Treasury that it purchased Iranian oil after Washington imposed sanctions on its Dalian subsidiary. The company insists it has never traded with Iran.

item.title: Trump Tells Iran to Call If It Wants to Negotiate as Peace Talks Stall
item.description: President Trump said Iran can reach out to the US if it wants to negotiate an end to the war, adding that US negotiators will no longer travel for talks. Both Tehran and Washington have shown little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Trump Was Likely Target of White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
item.description: A gunman opened fire at the White House Correspondents Association dinner, with officials saying Trump and his officials were likely targets. A Secret Service agent was wounded and the suspect was taken into custody.

item.title: Suspect in Press Dinner Shooting Wrote Anti-Christian Manifesto
item.description: The suspect in the White House Correspondents Dinner shooting, identified as Cole Allen, wrote an anti-Christian manifesto according to President Trump. The 31-year-old from California was armed with a shotgun.

item.title: King Charles State Visit to US to Proceed Despite Washington Shooting
item.description: Buckingham Palace confirmed King Charles state visit to the United States will go ahead as planned despite the shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner. The visit is scheduled for Monday.

item.title: Bennett and Lapid reunite to challenge Netanyahu in upcoming Israeli election
item.description: Former Israeli PMs Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid announced they are joining forces to oust Benjamin Netanyahus coalition government, highlighting their differences while presenting a united opposition front.

item.title: China unveils worlds first coal fuel cell with zero emissions
item.description: Chinese scientists developed a coal fuel cell that produces electricity with zero carbon emissions, achieving higher energy efficiency than traditional burning while eliminating pollution.

item.title: Mali Tuareg rebels agree deal for Russian Africa Corps withdrawal from Kidal
item.description: Tuareg rebels fighting Malis army announced an agreement allowing Russian Africa Corps forces to withdraw from Kidal region, as new fighting erupted in northern Mali.

item.title: US lawmaker proposes bipartisan commission on political violence after WHCA shooting
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna urged creation of a bipartisan national commission to address political violence following the shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner.

item.title: Italy to extradite Chinese hacker wanted by US authorities
item.description: Italy has decided to extradite a Chinese national wanted by the US for hacking charges including stealing COVID-19 medical research, marking a significant win for US cybercrime enforcement.

item.title: Pope Leo condemns warmongers as "thieves" on Chornobyl anniversary
item.description: On the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl disaster, Pope Leo issued a stark warning about nuclear power and condemned those who wage war, calling them thieves who steal humanity's peaceful future.

item.title: Americans oppose ending birthright citizenship, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
item.description: A majority of Americans believe all babies born in the US should automatically receive citizenship, as the Supreme Court prepares to rule on the contentious issue.

item.title: Trump Says He Holds Regular Talks With Putin and Zelenskiy on Ukraine War
item.description: US President Donald Trump revealed he maintains direct communication with both Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he pushes for a settlement to the ongoing conflict. The disclosure comes amid intensified diplomatic efforts.

item.title: Russian Defence Minister Visits North Korea in Second High-Level Trip
item.description: Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks, marking the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang in recent days. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang amid the Ukraine war.

item.title: Netanyahu's Main Political Rivals Unite to Challenge His Coalition
item.description: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's most formidable political rivals announced they are joining forces ahead of elections expected later this year, in a significant challenge to his long-standing coalition government.

item.title: Ukraine Marks 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under War Clouds
item.description: Ukraine commemorated four decades since the world's worst nuclear accident at Chornobyl, with officials warning that Russia's ongoing invasion could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

item.title: Israel Orders Evacuations of Seven Lebanese Towns After Soldier Killed
item.description: Israel issued evacuation warnings for seven towns in southern Lebanon beyond the Litani River buffer zone after a soldier was killed near the border. The move escalates tensions despite a fragile ceasefire with Hezbollah.

item.title: China Yuan Settlements Hit Record High Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: China Cross-border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) reached a record single-day transaction value of 1.22 trillion yuan, with analysts saying the Middle East conflict has acted as a catalyst for rising demand in yuan settlement, particularly in oil trade.

item.title: Germany Shifts Military Strategy Away From US Dependence
item.description: Germany new military doctrine emphasizes "less America, more Europe" as Berlin prepares for reduced US contributions to European security and takes greater responsibility for its own defense capabilities.

item.title: Two Israeli Soldiers Killed in Jordan Border Crossing Attack
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers were shot dead by a Jordanian aid truck driver near the Allenby (King Hussein) Crossing between the West Bank and Jordan. The attacker was neutralized at the site.

item.title: Iran foreign minister returns to Pakistan as US talks scrapped
item.description: Iran's top diplomat arrived back in Pakistan after planned talks with the US delegation were canceled. Trump says both sides can continue negotiations by phone as diplomatic efforts to end the Iran conflict continue.

item.title: Netanyahu orders army to attack Hezbollah in Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli PM directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah in Lebanon after six people were killed by strikes despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.
